channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Diplomatic Tensions Rise as China Sanctions Philippine Defense Secretary
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. criticized China's sanctions and verbal attacks on Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr., arguing that these actions are worsening bilateral relations. Marcos emphasize...

item.title: Pakistan government defends telecom bill amid allegations of corporate bias
item.description: The federal government has denied allegations that the Pakistan Telecommunication (Re-Organisation) Amendment Bill, 2026, was drafted to provide undue benefits to companies or individuals. Law Ministe...

item.title: Oyo State launches monthly cleanest market award to improve sanitation and market hygiene
item.description: The Oyo State Government has announced the introduction of the Cleanest Market of the Month Award, an initiative scheduled to begin in July 2026 as part of efforts to strengthen environmental sanitati...

item.title: Federal Government Denies Allegations of Spending Over N8 Trillion Outside Approved Budget
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don come out clearly to deny say dem spend more than N8 trillion outside the approved 2026 budget. This one na after IMF Resident Representative for Nigeria, Christia...

item.title: Weekly Superquiz Puzzle Challenge Now Live
item.description: The article promotes the Superquiz, a weekly puzzle challenge featuring a Mini Crossword with ten clues and twenty-five squares. It highlights the opportunity for premium subscribers of The Age and Th...

item.title: Tanzania's Women's Golf Vision and New Tournament Launch
item.description: The Tanzania Ladies Golf Union (TLGU) has introduced a four-year strategic plan to revolutionize women's golf in the country, shifting focus from competition to comprehensive development. The initiati...

item.title: Iran's Officials Attend Prayer for Late Supreme Leader Khamenei Amid Ongoing Tensions
item.description: Iran’s top officials and family members of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei attended funeral prayers in Tehran, signaling a shift in public safety perceptions amid escalating tensions wi...

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake: Over 3,000 Dead, Rescue Efforts Conclude
item.description: A devastating earthquake in Venezuela has resulted in nearly 3,000 fatalities, with rescue operations now concluding as international teams withdraw. The June 24 disaster, comprising two quakes measur...

item.title: Tony Elumelu's 2019 Roasted Corn Purchase Resurfaces Amid Small Business Discourse
item.description: A video showing billionaire Tony Elumelu buying roasted corn from a roadside vendor in Lagos, dated 2019, has resurfaced and gone viral after First Lady Oluremi Tinubu encouraged small businesses like...

item.title: Cheslin Kolbe Reflects on Springboks' Performance and Areas for Improvement
item.description: Cheslin Kolbe, the Springboks' try-scoring winger, acknowledged that his team is 'far from perfect' after their 45-21 victory over England in the Nations Championship opener. Despite the win, Kolbe hi...

item.title: Ondo North Senator calls for APC unity after disputed National Assembly primary election
item.description: The senator representing Ondo North Senatorial District, Olajide Ipinsagba, has appealed to aggrieved members of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Ondo State to accept the outcome of the party's ...

item.title: Wildfire Prediction Markets Spark Ethical Concerns Amid Rising Wildfire Risks
item.description: The article explores the controversial rise of wildfire prediction markets, where individuals bet on the outcomes of wildfires, including their spread, duration, and destruction. Platforms like Polyma...

item.title: Health Secretary Urges Legal Action Against Senior Staff Who Avoided Maternity Inquiry
item.description: The UK health secretary, James Murray, has announced plans to propose new legislation that would compel senior clinicians who refused to participate in the Ockenden Review into giving evidence. The re...

item.title: News Corp Australia Blocks Crawler Bots with Access Issues Message
item.description: The article discusses an automated message sent to users attempting to access News Corp Australia websites, indicating they may have been detected as a crawler bot. The message explains that News Corp...

item.title: EFF demands apology from Mbalula over defamatory remarks and threatens legal action
item.description: The Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) has issued a formal demand to ANC secretary-general Fikile Mbalula, requiring him to retract defamatory remarks made during an ANC media briefing. The party's lawye...

item.title: Private schools encouraged to diversify income and strengthen financial sustainability
item.description: Private school owners in Tanzania have been encouraged to reduce their dependence on tuition fees by developing additional income streams and exploring wider financing opportunities to improve long-te...

item.title: Green Man Festival marks a decade of helping refugees and asylum seekers gain skills and confidence through training placements
item.description: The Green Man Festival in Wales is celebrating the tenth anniversary of its refugee training programme, an initiative run by the Green Man Trust that has supported 191 refugees and asylum seekers as t...

item.title: Tanzania's DITF Drives Trade and Creates 600,000 Jobs Over 50 Years
item.description: The Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (DITF), also known as Sabasaba, has become a cornerstone of Tanzania's economic diplomacy over its 50-year history. Since its inception, the exhibition has g...

item.title: Masked assailant damages Israeli TV channel during escalating tensions
item.description: A masked assailant attacked the Israeli TV channel's premises, shattering glass and smashing doors, amid rising tensions in the region. The incident occurred as tensions between Israel and its neighbo...

item.title: Pakistan to Mediate US-Iran Technical Talks on July 14-15
item.description: Pakistan is set to host a fresh round of US-Iran technical talks from July 14-15, according to government sources. The discussions will focus on US sanctions on Iran, frozen assets, and the Iranian nu...
